title: Ixeris thunbergii
intro: Ixeris thunbergii, also known as Thunberg's dandelion, is a perennial herbaceous plant species belonging to the dandelion family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Korean and Japanese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixeris thunbergii include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Ixeris thunbergii is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote detoxification, reduce inflammation, and improve overall health.
taxonomy: Ixeris thunbergii is classified in the family Asteraceae. It is placed in the tribe Cichorieae, a group of dandelion-like plants. The genus Ixeris is often combined with the genus Crepis, forming the subtribe Crepideinae. However, some sources may group it under the subfamily Cichorioideae. It is native to East Asia, which contributes to its unique classification.
